postal.title: Postal Code Auto-fill Demo
postal.subtitle: Compare 4 ways to auto-fill prefecture and city from a 7-digit Japanese postal code.

core.desc: TypeScript-ready npm package. Fetches data via JSONP with in-session caching. Best compatibility with Nuxt 3.

core2.desc: Fork of yubinbango-core that adds module.exports = YubinBango for proper CommonJS export. No Vite plugin needed. Fully API-compatible.

zipcloud.desc: Calls the free zipcloud.ibsnet.co.jp API via $fetch. No npm install needed. Simple structure, easy to understand.

script.desc: CDN script tag version. Uses DOM class names (p-postal-code, p-region) that clash with Vue reactivity. Uses a setTimeout workaround. Shown as a counter-example.

composable.desc: Wraps yubinbango-core2 in a usePostalCode() composable for reuse. Share the same logic across multiple forms or projects.

index.title: Furigana Auto-fill Demo
index.subtitle: Compare 4 ways to auto-fill furigana (the kana reading) as you type a Japanese name through the IME.
index.howToTestBody: Click the Name field with Japanese IME active and type a name character by character. The furigana field fills in as you type through the IME.
index.howToTestNote: Uses compositionupdate to capture the kana reading before kanji conversion.

composable.desc: Custom composable wrapping IME events in composables/useAutoKana.ts. Zero deps, Vue-native. One line to use.

wanakana.desc: Binds to the furigana field via wanakana.bind(). Converts romaji to hiragana as you type. Not auto-fill from name — it's a furigana input helper.

inline.desc: Same logic as useAutoKana written directly in the component. Identical behavior, more boilerplate. Shows why the composable pattern exists.

naive.desc: v-model (input event) copies the value as-is. Gets kanji, not kana. Educational demo showing why compositionupdate is needed.

composable.desc: Wraps IME compositionupdate events in composables/useAutoKana.ts. Zero deps, Vue-native. One line to use.
pros: ✅ Zero deps — no npm install needed
✅ compositionupdate captures the IME reading before kanji conversion
✅ isKana check prevents overwrite when IME switches to candidate mode
✅ Accumulates kana across multiple composition segments

wanakana.desc: Binds to the furigana field via wanakana.bind(). Converts romaji input to hiragana in real time. Not auto-fill from name — it's a furigana input helper.
wanakana.warning: This page does NOT auto-fill from the Name field. Type romaji directly into the Furigana field — it converts to hiragana in real time.
pros: ⚠️ Not auto-fill from name — user types into the furigana field manually
⚠️ Does not use IME compositionupdate (string conversion only)
ℹ️ wanakana solves romaji→kana conversion, not IME-based auto-kana
wanakana.diff1: useAutoKana captures the IME reading of the Name field (田中太郎 → たなかたろう).
wanakana.diff2: wanakana.bind() converts romaji typed in the Furigana field (tanaka → たなか).
wanakana.diff3: Different UX — the former is fully automatic, the latter requires the user to type into the furigana field separately.

inline.desc: Same logic as useAutoKana written directly in the component without composable abstraction. Identical behavior, more boilerplate.
pros: ⚠️ Must write addEventListener setup in every component
⚠️ Copy-paste required if used in multiple forms
ℹ️ Moving this code to composables/useAutoKana.ts is the Composable version

naive.desc: Copies the input value as-is via v-model (input event). Gets kanji, not the kana reading. Educational demo showing why composition events are needed.
naive.warning: Intentionally broken. Type 田中太郎 in the Name field — the Furigana field gets 田中太郎 (kanji). This shows the limit of v-model (input event) without compositionupdate.
naive.errorMsg: ❌ Furigana got "{value}" instead of the kana reading. The IME reading was already lost by the time v-model (input event) fired.

why.body: Japanese IME converts in 3 steps: romaji → hiragana → kanji candidate. v-model / v-model (input event) only fires after kanji is committed — the hiragana reading is already gone at that point.
events: compositionupdate — kana reading is HERE (e.g. "たなか")
input — ← v-model / v-model (input event) fires here. Only kanji remains.
why.solution: Fix: listen to compositionupdate and record the reading while it's still kana. That's what useAutoKana Composable does.
